name: dff_with_const_undefined_input
description: Code should include a DFF cell with a constant undefined (x) value as its D input, which triggers removal optimization in opt_dff when the output feeds into another DFF
snippet:
cell $dff \dffb
    parameter \CLK_POLARITY 1
    parameter \WIDTH 1
    connect \CLK \clk
    connect \D 1'x
    connect \Q \qb
  end

name: dff_chain_with_feedback_wire
description: Code should include a chain of two DFFs where the first DFF's D input is the Q output of the second DFF, creating a feedback pattern that causes opt_dff to remove the second DFF while ModWalker still holds references
snippet:
cell $dff \dffa
    connect \D \qb
    connect \Q \qa
  end
  cell $dff \dffb
    connect \D 1'x
    connect \Q \qb
  end

name: opt_dff_sat_after_dff_chain
description: Code should include running opt_dff with -sat option on a design containing a chain of DFFs with undefined inputs, which exposes the use-after-free when cell removal occurs while ModWalker maintains stale references
snippet:
read_rtlil <<EOT
module \test
  wire \clk
  wire \qa
  wire \qb
  cell $dff \dffa
    connect \D \qb
    connect \Q \qa
  end
  cell $dff \dffb
    connect \D 1'x
    connect \Q \qb
  end
end
EOT
opt_dff -sat
